title: St. Mary's Church - Quick Summary
items: **St. Mary's Church is one of the oldest surviving churches in Berlin,** first documented in 1292 and still active near Alexanderplatz.
**Its strange setting is part of the story:** the church once stood inside the dense Marienviertel, not in today's open TV Tower landscape.
**The TV Tower contrast compresses Berlin's timeline:** medieval brick, GDR planning and modern tourism meet in one view.
**Inside, the Totentanz is the highlight:** a late-medieval Dance of Death fresco more than 22 metres long and about 2 metres high.
**Use it as the doorway into old Berlin:** connect it with the Rotes Rathaus, Nikolaiviertel, lost Marienviertel and Museum Island on foot.
